title: GIR (Geometric Intermediate Representation)
description: JSON schema for the Universal Language Geometric Intermediate Representation. GIR encodes meaning structures using 5 geometric primitives (Point, Line, Angle, Curve, Enclosure) mapped to 4 algebraic sorts (Entity, Relation, Modifier, Assertion).

root.description: ID of the root node in the containment hierarchy.

nodes.description: All geometric nodes in the structure.

binding_scope.description: Variable IDs currently in binding scope (set by bind operation).

modal_context.description: Modal context — tracks which nodes are possible worlds and which edges are accessibility relations.

shape.description: Enclosure shape — only meaningful for enclosure nodes.

directed.description: Whether the line is directed (arrow) — only meaningful for line nodes.

direction.description: Direction vector [dx, dy] — only meaningful for line nodes.

measure.description: Angle measure in degrees [0, 360) — only meaningful for angle nodes.

curvature.description: Constant curvature κ — only meaningful for curve nodes. When curvature_profile is present, this is the mean curvature.

curvature_profile.description: Piecewise curvature profile κ(s) sampled at uniform arc-length intervals. Overrides scalar curvature for variable-curvature curves.

vertices.description: Number of vertices — only meaningful for polygon enclosures.

variable_id.description: Variable identifier — only meaningful for variable_slot nodes and bound-reference nodes.

assertion_modifier.description: Assertion modifier kind — only meaningful for assertion-level modification.

force.description: Illocutionary force — only meaningful for assertion-sort enclosure nodes.

NodeType.description: The 5 geometric primitives of Universal Language plus variable_slot for binding.

EnclosureShape.description: Shape of an enclosure node. Circle=SO(2), Triangle=D₃, Square=D₄, Polygon=D_n (see vertices), Ellipse=bilateral, Freeform=no symmetry.

EdgeType.description: Geometric relationship between two nodes. accessible_from is used for modal accessibility relations.

AssertionModifierKind.description: Kind of assertion-level modification: evidential (epistemic source), emphatic (intensity), hedged (uncertainty).

world_nodes.description: Node IDs that represent possible worlds.

accessibility_edges.description: Indices into the edges array that are accessibility relations.

PerformativeForce.description: Illocutionary force parameter φ — what act the speaker performs with the assertion.
